title: Review standalone health workflow against real-life workflow patterns
summary: Review the standalone Philippines health coverage workflow product against many Real Life Workflows patterns, then produce an extensive source-grounded improvement research artifact and roadmap. Treat the standalone workflow as a product for people, not as a foundry-local workflow.
acceptance_criteria: The review treats `philippines-health-coverage` as the standalone product boundary, not as a foundry-local runtime.
The review compares the standalone health workflow against many Real Life Workflows doors and adjacent workflow patterns.
The review evaluates product usability, skill-pack shape, artifacts, evals, safety boundaries, installability, and user workflow fit.
The review separates shipped standalone product improvements from foundry-side manufacturing evidence.
The review preserves no-PHI, no-medical-advice, no-eligibility-decision, no-coverage-decision, no-claims-decision, and human-review boundaries.
The final artifact includes concrete improvements, prioritized next actions, and a rundown of what else can be built.
Repository workflow-state validation passes before any commit or push.
Any GitHub push happens only after explicit checkpoint approval.

context_snapshot.summary: Start from the completed standalone `philippines-health-coverage` workflow repo and Health project artifacts. Use Real Life Workflows only as source-grounded comparison research; do not create runtime dependency from the standalone health workflow back to the foundry or dependency project.

capability_dependencies.purpose: Compare the standalone health workflow product against many real-world workflow patterns and prepare source-grounded improvement evidence.

selected_skills.purpose: Search the Real Life Workflows source universe for healthcare, evidence-research, comparison, audit, document-packet, human-review, and public-service workflow patterns relevant to improving the standalone health workflow.
selected_skills.call_boundary: Use only as factory-side comparison research for this Health task. Do not create runtime dependency from the standalone health workflow back to real-life-workflows or this foundry.

dependency_steps.purpose: Create or reuse source-grounded real-world workflow comparison evidence before writing the standalone health workflow improvement review.
expected_inputs: completed Health project artifacts from health-002, health-004, and health-005
need to compare against many real-world workflow patterns, not only healthcare examples
no PHI, no medical advice, no coverage decisions, no claims decisions, and human-review boundaries
expected_outputs: workflow-understanding packets under projects/health/artifacts/workflow-packets/ after phase approval
extensive standalone product review under projects/health/artifacts/reviews/ after phase approval
prioritized improvement roadmap under projects/health/artifacts/plans/ after phase approval

promotion_rules: Use Real Life Workflows results only as source-grounded comparison evidence.
Do not create runtime dependency from the standalone health workflow to this foundry or to real-life-workflows.
Keep implementation recommendations as foundry review artifacts until a later task applies them to the standalone repo.
Do not mutate the standalone repo during this review task.
stop_conditions: Stop if a dependency call attempts to mutate real-life-workflows tracker or skill files.
Stop if a write targets the standalone repo instead of the approved Health review artifacts.
Stop if recommendations require PHI handling, medical advice, coverage decisions, claims decisions, rankings, or purchase recommendations.

dependency_artifacts.purpose: Create source-grounded workflow comparison evidence for reviewing the standalone Philippines health coverage workflow product.

promotion_metadata.promotion_reason: Used as official comparison evidence for the standalone health workflow product review.

inputs: standalone public-source health coverage workflow product evidence research comparison navigator audit human review document packet public service
existing Health artifacts from health-002, health-004, and health-005
